articles.original_title: Skenes pitches with poise through pressure in WBC spotlight, treats it same as Pirates outings
articles.summary: Paul Skenes pitched with poise through pressure, dispatching a series of stars on a night of great stakes in a sold-out stadium with millions watching on TV.  This was the type of game he never sees with the Pittsburgh Pirates and he may not experience on a regular basis until 2030. articles.title: Dominican WBC loss ends on called strike that appeared low, a week before robot umps arrive in MLB
articles.original_title: Dominican WBC loss ends on called strike that appeared low, a week before robot umps arrive in MLB
articles.summary: Ten days later, the United States and the Dominican Republic would have kept on playing.  Geraldo Perdomo watched Mason Miller's full-count slider appear to drop just under the strike zone and took a step toward his team's dugout on the third-base side, thinking he walked to put runners at the corners.  Then plate umpire Cory Blaser emphatically signaled strike three, stranding the potential tying run at third base and giving the United States a 2-1 win Sunday night that advanced the Americans t

articles.title: Knicks have first quarter problem. Is a change to starting five the answer?
articles.original_title: Knicks have first quarter problem. Is a change to starting five the answer?
articles.summary: Since the All-Star break, the Knicks’ starting five has been outscored by a combined 34 points in the first quarter. On Sunday night, those struggles nearly cost them against a Warriors team playing without eight key rotation players.
